# AbstractFlow LLM Guide

> AbstractFlow is the web visual workflow editor for AbstractFramework.

Use this repo as an npm/web package:

- Package: `@abstractframework/flow`
- Source: `src/`
- CLI/proxy: `bin/cli.js`
- Examples: `examples/flows/`
- Docs: `docs/`

Do not assume a Python package, FastAPI backend, or local execution host exists in AbstractFlow. Execution, provider configuration, user auth, workflow persistence, runs, ledgers, artifacts, and runtime isolation are owned by AbstractGateway and AbstractRuntime.

Useful files:

- [README](README.md)
- [Getting started](docs/getting-started.md)
- [Web editor](docs/web-editor.md)
- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md)
- [API and contracts](docs/api.md)
- [VisualFlow JSON](docs/visualflow.md)
- [CLI](docs/cli.md)
- [FAQ](docs/faq.md)
- [Gateway client](src/utils/gatewayClient.ts)
- [Gateway catalog helpers](src/utils/gatewayCatalog.ts)
- [Run modal](src/components/RunFlowModal.tsx)
- [Connection modal](src/components/GatewayConnectionModal.tsx)
- [Node templates](src/types/nodes.ts)

Current media/editor behavior:

- Gateway is the only execution boundary. Flow may capture browser-local files
  or microphone audio, but uploads them to Gateway as artifacts before
  start/resume.
- Unconnected artifact input pins expose node-level upload controls.
- Direct image, image-edit, video, and image-to-video child-run progress is
  rendered from Gateway `abstract.progress` ledger records when available.
- Connection previews must use canonical pin types; execution handles only
  preview/connect to execution handles.

Validation:

```bash
npm run build
npm run lint
npm run docs:llms
```
